visualGoal: Make a rally/drift builder buy the RIGHT type for their discipline and mount it rigidly. The article's core products (handbrakes + shifters) have NO verified photo yet, so current visuals anchor on the verified mounting/cockpit + pedals catalog because mounting is the article's central thesis. Top priority for Codex: source real handbrake + shifter product photos so those units can appear as gearpick/shotgrid asides. No procedural art, no stand-in images.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Budget/analog handbrake pick ($99, 16-bit non-contact Hall angle sensor). Currently text-only because no verified image. Highest-priority photo for this article — it is the default first handbrake recommendation.

Console-friendly load-cell pick (~$200, launched Dec 2025, PC/PS5/Xbox). NOTE: internal product data still carries the OLD V1.5 potentiometer image path (/images/gear/racing/fanatec-clubsport-handbrake-v15.jpg) which is a DIFFERENT product — source a V2 photo, do not reuse the V1.5.
Value load-cell pick (~$175, 150kg cell, swappable elastomers, two-stage damper). No gear page yet — consider adding one.
Pro/endgame load-cell handbrake (~$400, 120kg cell, SmartControl). Confirm exact US price and the direct product URL before certifying.
Best sequential pick ($129, non-contact Hall, USB/MOZA base). Currently text-only. Second-highest photo priority after the MOZA HBP.
Best switchable/H-pattern value pick (100% metal, PC/console). Current price unconfirmed this pass — body says check current.
Premium switchable shifter (~$300, full metal). Verify the gear-page slug and official-spec URL resolve; confirm current MSRP.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic sim-racing cockpit at amber rim light, a rigid 8020 aluminum-profile rig with side rails, an empty bolt-down side plate left open for a real handbrake product overlay and a second plate for a sequential shifter, low and slightly forward of the seat where a hand falls off the wheel, cable tray visible, curator Duke Alvarez gesturing at the rigid mount point, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake handbrake, fake shifter, warped controls, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art, desk clamp on particle board shown as good

infographicPrompts.title: Handbrake + shifter by discipline
content: Rally: load-cell handbrake first, sequential second
Drift: load-cell handbrake non-negotiable, sequential second
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center decision chart, large readable text, racing colors, discipline icons per row

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult Gen X sim-racing curator Duke Alvarez in a clean garage cockpit bay, one hand pulling a rig-mounted handbrake to show zero flex, confident mechanic energy, product overlay space beside the rail, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.
